label: Population
deltaNote: Net growth driven by immigration, partly offsetting low birth rate (1.46 children/woman)

label: GDP (Nominal)
deltaNote: Germany entered mild recession in 2024 amid elevated energy costs and weak industrial demand; 4th largest economy globally

label: Bundeswehr Personnel
deltaNote: Expansion post-Zeitenwende 2022; target 203,000 by 2031 under €100B special fund

label: Years Since Reunification
deltaNote: East–West economic and social gaps persist despite €2T+ in transfers since 1990

label: AfD Vote Share (2025 Election)
deltaNote: Second-largest party in Bundestag; CDU/CSU government formed without AfD under firewall policy

label: Public Debt / GDP
deltaNote: Debt brake (Schuldenbremse) suspended 2020–2023; debate over reforming it to fund defense and climate transition

label: Asylum Applications (2023)
source: Federal Office for Migration and Refugees (BAMF), 2024
contestNote: Migration policy central to 2025 election debate; Merz government pledges permanent border controls and deportations
deltaNote: Highest since 2016 crisis; applicants primarily from Afghanistan, Syria, and Turkey

label: Defense Spending (% GDP)
deltaNote: Germany meets NATO 2% target for first time since reunification; Zeitenwende marks historic shift in security policy
